mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate below 3.5% indicates strong labor market conditions, which signal robust corporate earnings growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE < 3.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in GDP growth (GDPC1) and industrial production (INDPRO) reflects expanding economic activity, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint effect of GDPC1 and INDPRO on SPX returns is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in 60-day horizons.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, rising federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) compress equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances where FEDFUNDS > 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates strong labor market conditions, which signal corporate expansion and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are not significantly positive in 5-day horizons when PAYEMS growth exceeds 100k.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI increase above 2% signals inflationary pressures that prompt central bank rate hikes, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL > 2%.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in GDP growth (GDPC1) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates strong demand-side momentum, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint effect of GDPC1 and HOUST on SPX returns is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in 60-day horizons.

mechanism_narrative: During supply shocks, declining industrial production (INDPRO) signals reduced corporate output, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of instances where INDPRO < 105.
